summary: Joel Spolsky briefly recounts experiencing his first earthquake, a 5.6 magnitude event centered 9 miles away while staying at a hotel. He describes the shaking lasting about 15 seconds and observes lights flashing in a nearby Adobe building. He compares the experience to airplane turbulence.
key_points: Joel experienced his first earthquake, a 5.6 magnitude event near his hotel
The Adobe building across the road had lights flashing on and off during the shaking
He compares the sensation to being on an airplane during turbulence
money_quotes: the whole hotel shook for about 15 seconds
I guess it's sort of like being on an airplane during turbulance
key_insight: A brief personal anecdote about experiencing a first earthquake, with no broader technical or professional insight.
